frontmatter.title: An Extension of Models of Axiomatic Domain Theory to Models of Synthetic Domain Theory

mainmatter.p: We relate certain models of Axiomatic Domain Theory (ADT) and Synthetic Domain Theory (SDT). On the one hand, we introduce a class of non-elementary models of SDT and show that the domains in them yield models of ADT. On the other hand, for each model of ADT in a wide class we construct a model of SDT such that the domains in it provide a model of ADT which conservatively extends the original model.

frontmatter.title: Using Synthetic Methods to Prove Analytical Facts

mainmatter.p: Today when synthetic methods are employed, it is usually with the intention of proving something about a concrete (analytically constituted) object. For instance, synthetic differential geometry is used to prove things about actual smooth manifolds; synthetic domain theory can be used to prove things about actual \(\omega\)-cpos. Likewise, synthetic Tait computability can be used to prove things about the actual syntax of actual programming languages!
